data.title: Arcutis wins FDA nod for Zoryve foam for plaque psoriasis
data.description:
• Arcutis Biotherapeutics (NASDAQ:ARQT) announced Thursday that the U.S. FDA approved a topical foam version of its plaque psoriasis therapy Zoryve (roflumilast) at 0.3% to treat scalp and body conditions associated with the skin disorder.
  • The decision related to the company’s supplemental New Drug Application ((sNDA)) targeted at FDA approval for Zoryve 0.3% to treat scalp and body lesions in patients aged 12 years and above with plaque psoriasis.
  • Previously, the company had regulatory clearance in the U.S. to market the once-daily therapy for a skin disorder known as seborrheic dermatitis in those aged nine years and older.
  • The decision was based on the company’s Phase 2 and pivotal Phase 3 trials designed to evaluate the anti-inflammatory agent in plaque psoriasis, and it marked the fifth FDA approval for Zoryve in less than three years.

data.title: TSLA, DELL, UBER: New leaders amid market rotation?
data.description:
A significant market (DJI), (DIA) rotation is underway as investors shift away from recent leading stocks toward previous underperformers, according to Bank of America research.

The analysis identifies bullish trends and breakout patterns in three key sectors, including industrials (XLI), information technology (XLK), and consumer discretionary (XLY).

 

 

The industrial sector (XLI) has demonstrated a relative breakout while outperforming during the broader market recovery, said Paul Ciana, head of FICC technical strategy research at BofA.

He highlighted Uber (NYSE:UBER), which carries the second-largest weight in the industrials index (XLI), as particularly promising. “Uber’s 18-month trading range has broken bullishly,” Ciana said. The company shows technical indicators supporting potential price targets of $101.50-113.60.

In addition, the information technology sector (XLK) displays strength across three of four key technical indicators. Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) stands out despite being a smaller component of the index, with analysts projecting it could rally to previous peaks of $145 and potentially reach $175.

“If Dell holds support above $100 and breaks its trend channel, the setup remains bullish,” Ciana said.

Lastly, the consumer discretionary sector (XLY) similarly shows positive readings across three of four key measures. Within the sector, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA), which holds the second-largest weight in this index, features prominently in the research due to its bullish triangle pattern.

The report emphasizes that Tesla’s (NASDAQ:TSLA) setup “looks bullish while above $300,” with a recent bottoming near $200 and a bullish gap into the $300s that analysts interpret as a breakaway signal.

BofA analysts express confidence in these ongoing trends based on various technical indicators and price movements within the broader market shift. The research positions Uber (NYSE:UBER), Dell (NYSE:DELL), and Tesla (TSLA) as potential market leaders moving forward as the rotation continues to unfold.

“These stocks and sectors show the strongest technical setups for continued outperformance.”

data.title: Wall Street holding on to gains as U.S. bond sell-off eases up
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages pushed higher on Thursday, while a bruising bond sell-off from the previous session eased up. The focus remained on President Trump's "One Big Beautiful" tax bill, which was narrowly passed by the U.S. House.

Stock index futures showed little reaction to the favorable vote, and the major averages eventually opened mixed. Moves have remained small since. The benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) was last +0.40% at 5,867.88 points in late afternoon trade, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.44% at 42,044.82 points. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.74% at 19,011.41 points.

U.S. Treasury yields had stabilized on Thursday after soaring on Wednesday, as traders had dumped bonds amid concerns over the new tax bill's effects on the ballooning U.S. fiscal deficit. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was last down 5 basis points to 4.56%, while the shorter-end more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 2 basis points to 4.01%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.  

Following days of marathon meetings and intense negotiations, including Republican efforts that lasted through the night, lawmakers early this morning voted 215-214 in favor of the bill that contains more than 1,000 pages and proposes about $3.8T in tax cuts, including the extension of those enacted by U.S. President Trump's Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (TCJA) in 2017. 

But worries over the implications of the bill on the U.S. fiscal deficit have been on top of investors' minds.

"The die was cast here the moment both presidential tickets agreed to extend TCJA. That keeps revenues at 17% GDP as spending continues inexorable rise & deficits in 6-7% range. The issue is that GOP has taken off the table vast majority of spending progs. (Democrats) have no plan either," Donald Schneider, deputy head of U.S. policy at Piper Sandler, said on X (formerly Twitter) on Wednesday.

"GOP is doing something quite difficult now which is enacting real entitlement cuts and real tax increases. So they are demonstrating credibility that it can be done. But no one should be surprised that deficits will linger in 6%+ territory," Schneider said. 

"Fiscal outlook is basically set at this point. Resolution of the crisis will be left to next President in 2029. Unless both parties do total 180 on entitlement reform," he added. 

Though the tax bill dominated headlines on Thursday, there was also some economic data to focus on. The number of Americans filing for initial jobless claims in the past week slipped. Meanwhile, S&P Global's flash survey for May showed U.S. business activity growth and expectations for future output improving from April lows. Additionally, U.S. existing home sales unexpectedly dipped in April. 

Turning to active movers, solar stocks were crushed as the House passage of the tax bill hastens a potential end to key clean energy credits. Enphase Energy (ENPH) slumped -19.4% and was the top percentage loser on the S&P 500 (SP500).

data.title: Nu Holdings slashes management layers, CEO says
data.description:
Nu Holdings (NYSE:NU), a digital bank that operates under the NuBank name, cut its number of management layers by half as it seeks to increase efficiency and stay nimble, its CEO said in a media interview on Thursday.

The company had grown "too verticalized," expanding to as many as 14 layers, CEO and co-founder David Velez said in an interview with Bloomberg News.

The streamlining came as several top executives left the firm, including Jag Duggal and Youssef Lahrech.

"There were four layers between me and the senior managers of the products, and like 14 layers across the organization, which is too much for an organization that needs to be agile, make decisions quickly, and has so much growth ahead," he said. 

Nu (NYSE:NU) has reduced the layers by half and "significantly" increased the speed at which it moves, he added. The company has more than 118M clients in Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia, and plans to expand internationally. To help with that initiative, it will hire new c-suite executives.

Just this week, Lahrech stepped down as president and chief operating officer. His departure follows those of Duggal, who was the chief product officer, investor relations head Jorg Friedemann, and Chief Legal Officer Elita Ariaz. While Velez understands that the exits look scary to investors, the company is "bringing in a new set of executives for the new phase."

There haven't been any layoffs, and there are no plans for any, Velez said. The revamping is focused on execution and efficiency rather than cost-cutting. 

Nu Holdings (NU) stock rose 2.8% in Thursday afternoon trading.

data.title: Guess? unveils a licensee partnership to produce premium eyewear under for the rag & bone brand
data.description:
Guess? (NYSE:GES) is making a stronger move into the premium eyewear business in partnership with WHP Global through a four-year exclusive licensee partnership with Marcolin, which is a longtime licensee partner of the company, as well as producer and distributor of eyewear.

WHP Global jointly owns the New York-based fashion brand rag & bone with Guess? (NYSE:GES). The new eyewear collection will be available at rag & bone boutiques, rag-bone.com, and in select eyewear stores and authorized retailers.

Since its origins in New York in 2002, rag & bone was highlighted as establishing itself as a leader in the American fashion scene. The brand was said to be known for its authentic understated New York aesthetic, blending traditional craftsmanship with modern cultural references. Notably, rag & bone eyewear is inspired by the "energy" of the metropolitan lifestyle. 

Guess? (GES) has been working with the team from Marcolin for over 30 years on delivering fashionable trend-right eyewear to the market. "We are thrilled to expand that partnership to rag & bone further growing the lifestyle of the brand through their expertise, commitment to style and innovation," stated Chief Creative Officer Paul Marciano.

data.title: Williams-Sonoma reiterates flat to 3% comp revenue growth for 2025 while advancing AI and supply chain efficiencies
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (WSM) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Laura Alber, President and CEO, opened by stating, "We are proud to deliver strong results in the first quarter of 2025, driven by a positive top-line comp and continued strength in our profitability." She highlighted a comp of 3.4%, operating margin of 16.8%, and earnings per share of $1.85, with earnings growth of 8.8%. Alber noted, "Our growing outperformance was driven by an improvement in furniture sales, effective collaborations, and strong performance in our retail and e-commerce channels."
  • Alber emphasized three key priorities: "returning to growth, elevating our world-class customer service, and driving earnings." She underscored strategic collaborations, B2B program growth (8% in Q1), and emerging brands showing strong comps as drivers, while highlighting investments in AI across digital platforms and supply chain.
  • Alber stated, "We are reiterating the same outlook that we shared with you last quarter," addressing ongoing macroeconomic and tariff uncertainties. The six-point plan for managing tariffs was outlined, including vendor cost concessions, resourcing to lower-tariff countries, supply chain efficiencies, SG&A expense controls, expanding made-in-USA assortment, and selective price increases.
  • Jeff Howie, CFO, said, "We are proud to have delivered Q1 results exceeding expectations on both the top and bottom lines." Howie added, "Q1 net revenues finished at $1.73 billion at a positive 3.4% comp, with all brands delivering positive comps in the quarter."

Outlook

  • Management reiterated guidance for fiscal 2025: comp brand revenue growth of flat to positive 3% and operating margin in the range of 17.4% to 17.8%.
  • Alber noted, "Our guidance reflects that which we know today, and we are not assuming any significant upside or downside from broader macroeconomic factors."
  • Howie confirmed, "We expect 2025 net revenue comps to be in the range of flat to positive 3%, with total net revenues in a range of down 1.5% to positive 1.5% due to the 53rd week impact from '24."
  • Guidance incorporates current tariffs, including the China tariff at 30% and the global reciprocal tariff at 10%, with the six-point mitigation plan anticipated to offset these costs.

Financial Results

  • Net revenues were reported at $1.73 billion, with a positive 3.4% comp across all brands.
  • Retail channel delivered a 6.2% comp; e-commerce was up 2.1%.
  • Gross margin for Q1 2025 was 44.3%, with a noted 60 basis point year-over-year decline (excluding last year’s out-of-period adjustment) driven by merchandise margins, supply chain efficiencies, and occupancy.
  • SG&A was 27.5% of revenues, 130 basis points lower than last year, attributed to tight cost control and lower advertising expense.
  • Operating margin stood at 16.8%, and EPS was $1.85.
  • Cash balance was $1 billion with no outstanding debt; merchandise inventories at $1.3 billion, up 10% due to a strategic pull forward of receipts to mitigate tariff impact.
  • Capital expenditures were $58 million in the quarter, and $165 million was returned to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends.

Q&A

  • Brad Thomas, KeyBanc: Asked about merchandise margin decline and promotional strategy. Howie responded, "Our promotional stance has not changed...our penetration of full-price selling slightly increased in the quarter." Supply chain efficiencies and occupancy leverage were also highlighted.
  • Peter Benedict, Baird: Queried pricing philosophy amidst tariffs. Alber explained, value is determined by design, quality, brand, and service, not just price, and selective price increases have been implemented where appropriate.
  • Maksim Rakhlenko, TD Cowen: Asked about merchandise margins trajectory. Howie reiterated the company does not guide to specific lines but is confident in maintaining guidance due to the mitigation plan.
  • Jonathan Matuszewski, Jefferies: Sought color on demand trends and comp cadence. Howie stated all brands delivered positive comps and emphasized market share gains.
  • Cristina Fernandez, Telsey: Inquired about reducing China exposure. Alber said, "We've already significantly reduced our China source of goods from 50% to 23% over the last few years," and flexibility remains to further adapt.
  • Seth Sigman, Barclays: Asked about inventory position. Howie confirmed the increase was strategic, intended to mitigate tariffs, and in-stocks and on-time deliveries are at all-time highs.
  • Simeon Gutman, Morgan Stanley: Queried expectations for comp improvement and tariff costs. Alber focused on long-term growth and market share gains, while Howie detailed short-term expenses and future benefits from mitigation actions.
  • Christopher Horvers, JPMorgan: Asked about the impact of consumer behavior and tariff pull forward. Howie attributed performance to company strategies and innovation rather than pull-forward effects.
  • Emily Ghosh, Goldman Sachs: Requested details on supply chain efficiencies. Alber highlighted cost reductions from lower returns and improved delivery metrics.
  • Unidentified Analyst (for Robby Ohmes, BofA): Asked about B2B contribution. Alber sees B2B as a $2 billion opportunity.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was generally neutral, with a focus on margin sustainability, tariff impacts, and inventory management, seeking clarity on execution and risk.
  • Management maintained a confident, slightly optimistic tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A. Alber and Howie repeatedly referenced the company’s ability to adapt, stating, "We are confident in our ability to adapt and navigate whatever lies ahead."
  • Compared to last quarter, management’s tone was consistent, with continued focus on flexibility and market share gains, while analysts remained focused on operational details and potential headwinds.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language was unchanged, reiterating flat to 3% comp revenue growth and 17.4% to 17.8% operating margin.
  • Strategic focus deepened on AI integration and supply chain efficiencies, with new details on tariff mitigation.
  • Analysts continued to probe on tariffs, margin sustainability, and demand trends, similar to prior quarter.
  • Key metric changes: Q1 2025 comp improved to 3.4% from Q4's 3.1%; operating margin decreased from Q4 due to seasonality but was up year-over-year excluding prior adjustments.
  • Management’s confidence remained steady, emphasizing execution and adaptability, while analysts’ tone stayed neutral with focus on potential risks.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainties, ongoing consumer distraction with tariffs, and a soft housing market as risks.
  • Tariff impacts were clearly addressed, with mitigation via a six-point plan, including vendor negotiations, resourcing, and selective price increases.
  • Analysts pressed on potential margin pressures, inventory risks, and demand sustainability, reflecting ongoing sector headwinds.

Final Takeaway

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. management emphasized that strong Q1 results were achieved through positive comps across all brands, disciplined cost control, and strategic mitigation of tariff impacts. With guidance for comp brand revenue growth of flat to positive 3% and operating margin of 17.4% to 17.8% reaffirmed, the company remains focused on growth, customer service, and earnings, while leveraging its vertically integrated model, digital transformation, and robust supply chain to navigate uncertainty and drive shareholder value in 2025.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Planet Fitness flexing its muscles with new members, Black Card perks - analyst
data.description:
With “considerable runway for further improvement” and potential for increased membership thanks to Black Card benefits, Stifel’s Chris O’Cull upgraded Planet Fitness (NYSE:PLNT) to Buy from Hold and raised his price target by 46% to $120.

The upgrade extended the stock’s winning streak to a seventh consecutive day with a new 3-month-high.

“Recent results suggest gross joins have stabilized, and we believe there are several potential catalysts to keep comparable sales in the mid-to-high single-digit range over the next couple of years,” O’Cull said in his research note.

Advertising campaigns to emphasize Planet Fitness (NYSE:PLNT) as an affordable and judge-free gym should fuel growth over the next few years, as well as the increased price of its Black Card monthly membership.

But in addition to a higher fee, the Black Card membership offers other avenues of growth, including spa services, spray tanning, red light therapy, and cryotherapy services.

Planet Fitness’ low-cost membership model of $15 per month “should also prove resilient in the event of a downturn in consumer spending,” O’Cull adds, evidenced by a 900K increase in members. 

And thanks to the “We’re All Strong on This Planet” marketing campaign, the improved brand perception contributed to a 30% rejoin rate.

This helped the company achieve 11.6% growth in revenue and increased profitability. Unfortunately, both missed Wall Street’s lofty goals, knocking shares lower before the stock recouped its losses and is now trading 14% higher than the post-earnings low.

Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors all share a Buy rating on Planet Fitness (PNT). Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, however, views the stock as a Hold with a 3.15 Quant score out of 5.

data.title: Shares in TV adtech firm MNTN soar in IPO debut
data.description:
MNTN Inc. (NYSE:MNTN) stock opened at $21 in its IPO debut on the New York Stock Exchange on Thursday, 31.3% above the offering price of $16. 

The company, which specializes in connected TV advertising and has actor Ryan Reynolds as its creative chief, offered 11.7M Class A shares and aimed to raise $187.2M. It currently has a market cap of $1.2B.

The stock hit a high of $26.29, rising as much as 64%, and saw a trading volume of over 8M shares by 2:00 pm ET.

According to its latest SEC filing, the company reported revenue of $64.5M and a net loss of $21.1M, or $1.41 per share, for the three months ended March 31.

In Q1, its performance TV revenue saw a 48% rise from last year and recorded customer growth of 89% in the segment. As of 2024, the company's TV ads have targeted 130M households.

In March, Seeking Alpha contributor and Investing Group leader  Donovan James wrote that "MNTN is benefiting from a continued migration of television consumers from linear TV to connected TV offerings."

James said consumer behavior is changing toward multiscreen activities, increasing the likelihood of shopping while watching CTV, which would be a positive for MNTN. He also thinks advertisers will see better ROI as contextual targeting of ads improves with AI.

Baroda Ventures, Mercato Partners, Greycroft, Qualcomm, MGD Holdings, Bonfire Ventures, Blackrock, Fidelity, and Peak Investment Holdings are among the 5% stockholders in the company. 

Morgan Stanley, Citigroup, Evercore ISI, Raymond James, Citizens Capital Markets, Susquehanna, Needham, Loop Capital, and Tigress Financial were among the underwriters to the IPO.

data.title: Enersys signals Q1 2026 revenue low point; targets margin growth amid tariff and macro headwinds
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: EnerSys (ENS) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO David Shaffer stated EnerSys delivered a strong quarter, growing revenue 7% to reach its second highest revenue quarter and achieving record adjusted diluted EPS of $1.86 excluding 45X benefits. He highlighted record Motive Power margins, significant margin expansion in Energy Systems and Specialty, and strong Bren-Tronics acquisition contributions. "We executed our strategy in a challenging environment. We expanded our share in the attractive and growing defense market, grew our higher-margin maintenance-free offerings, reduced cost, optimized our manufacturing footprint, invested in high speed, lower cost flexible domestic production capacity and developed new product offerings, strengthening our foundation for future growth," said Shaffer.
  • President, COO & Incoming CEO Shawn O'Connell explained a new strategic roadmap is being finalized, focused on deeper customer engagement in energy security and labor scarcity, narrowing focus on select growth verticals, expanding service, and achieving further operational efficiencies. He stated, "We are committed to fully mitigating any financial impact to our shareholders and see OpEx reductions as an effective lever for what cannot be offset by supply or pricing actions."
  • O'Connell discussed tariff risk mitigation, noting a cross-functional task force is in place and that at current tariff levels, direct exposure is approximately $92 million. He added, "We intend to fully offset this impact, but expect some near term friction in Q1 due to stranded tariffs that can't be passed on to customers and shifting customer order patterns."
  • Executive Vice President & CFO Andrea Funk reported, "Fourth quarter net sales of $975 million were up 7% from prior year, the second highest revenue quarter for the company, driven by a 4% increase in organic volume...and a 4% positive impact from the Bren-Tronics acquisition, partially offset by 2% FX headwind. We achieved adjusted gross profit of $304 million, up $49 million year-on-year."

Outlook

  • Andrea Funk guided for Q1 fiscal 2026 net sales in the range of $830 million to $870 million and adjusted diluted EPS of $2.03 to $2.13 per share. She said, "The largest driver of our anticipated Q1 sequential decline in revenue and EPS can be attributed to Motive Power volumes, which are expected to be pressured on seasonality and tariff disruptions with Motive Power Americas Q4 orders down 14% year-on-year as well as to a lesser extent approximately $5 million of stranded tariff costs."
  • Management paused quantified full-year guidance due to evolving policy environment: "Given the evolving policy environment and pacing of demand normalization, we are temporarily pausing quantified full year guidance. That said, we believe Q1 will be the low point of our fiscal year."
  • Funk stated, "We anticipate full year adjusted operating earnings growth excluding 45X benefits to outpace revenue growth for the fiscal year."

Financial Results

  • EnerSys reported adjusted gross margin of 31.2% and adjusted operating earnings of $152 million for Q4, with an adjusted operating margin of 15.6%.
  • Adjusted EPS for the fourth quarter was $2.97 per share; excluding 45X, adjusted EPS was $1.86 per share, up $0.66 from the prior year.
  • Energy Systems Q4 revenue increased 8% to $399 million, with adjusted operating earnings of $35 million and margin of 8.7%. Motive Power revenue was $392 million, with adjusted operating earnings of $67 million and margin of 17.1%. Specialty revenue rose 21% to $178 million, with $15 million in adjusted operating earnings and margin of 8.5%.
  • Positive operating cash flow of $135 million and free cash flow of $105 million were generated in Q4. The company ended the year with $343 million in cash and cash equivalents and net debt of $781 million.

Q&A

  • Noah Kaye, Oppenheimer: Asked about Q1 revenue and EPS guidance, and drivers of EPS growth on flat revenues. Shawn O'Connell explained, "We anticipate that this blip that we saw was totally tariff shock related and that the markets are largely getting back to business...we feel very good about continued trajectory in ES and a rising comfort level in Motive."
  • Andrea Funk clarified that Q1 EPS is about $0.10 higher year-on-year, with normalization from Bren-Tronics and 45X, offset by FX pressure, resulting in flat EPS year-on-year after adjustments. She noted, "We'd expect without a doubt that Q1 is going to be the low point and we should see recovery going on from there, absent additional tariff shocks."
  • Funk explained the decision to pause full-year guidance: "The main reason we're just not in a position to provide annual guidance right now is just awaiting the final outcome of the reciprocal tariff negotiations and how that's all going to shake out."
  • Greg Lewis, BTIG: Asked about M&A opportunities and leverage. Funk stated, "No bolt-ons at all in our number at all." O'Connell added, "We still see a very long runway in A&D...we're going to be very opportunistic with that dry powder and stay acquisitive as long as those targets fit our ROIC model."
  • Chip Moore, ROTH Capital Partners: Asked about Energy Systems green shoots and last mile communications. O'Connell responded, "We're starting to see the backbone enhanced and some of that investment going in."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was inquisitive but cautious, with repeated questions on order book recovery, tariff impacts, and the company's ability to maintain or restore guidance.
  • Management maintained a confident and disciplined tone, frequently referencing scenario planning, risk mitigation, and structural advantages. O'Connell stated, "We are prepared for a range of outcomes guided by our proven and refined playbook."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted to more cautious language around guidance and macro uncertainty, while emphasizing operational resilience and readiness.
  • Analysts pressed for clarity on order book trends and guidance, indicating slightly more skepticism than the prior quarter.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for full-year was paused this quarter due to tariff and macro uncertainty, while last quarter included specific full-year and Q4 guidance.
  • Q4 saw a step-up in adjusted EPS and margins, with record adjusted diluted EPS excluding 45X benefits, compared to Q3's sequentially lower EPS and margin expansion.
  • Management focus shifted from recovery optimism and specific revenue growth targets last quarter to risk management, cost controls, and order volatility this quarter.
  • Analyst concerns evolved from demand normalization and FX/commodity impacts in Q3 to the sustainability of order recovery and clarity on macro/policy impacts in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited tariff exposure as a challenge, with $92 million in direct exposure at current rates, and some near-term Q1 friction expected due to stranded tariffs and shifting customer order patterns.
  • Order volatility and macroeconomic uncertainty, especially around tariffs and reciprocal trade policy, drive temporary demand fluctuations.
  • Specialty segment faces delays in Class 8 truck recovery due to macro and tariff uncertainty, and some near-term delays in A&D orders attributed to timing issues from personnel reductions in administration.
  • Inventory dynamics and service lag are noted as risks, but management emphasized structural buffers and scenario planning.

Final Takeaway

EnerSys management underscored that despite the near-term challenges from tariff exposure, order volatility, and paused full-year guidance, the business remains fundamentally strong and well-positioned for margin and earnings growth, with Q1 2026 expected to be the low point for the fiscal year. The company is focused on disciplined execution, risk mitigation, and leveraging its flexible manufacturing and product innovation capabilities to capitalize on growth opportunities once macro and policy uncertainties subside.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Blue Owl, Crusoe, Primary Digital Infrastructure to get $7.1B loan for AI data center development
data.description:
Newmark Group (NASDAQ:NMRK) said on Thursday that it arranged a $7.1B construction loan for Blue Owl Capital (NYSE:OWL), Crusoe, and Primary Digital Infrastructure for the development of an AI data center.

The three companies announced the second phase of the $15B joint venture yesterday.

The loan will be used to fund the second phase of a $15B joint venture to finance the 1.2-gigawatt AI data center in Abilene, Texas.

As part of the joint venture, Crusoe, Blue Owl and Primary Digital Infrastructure will jointly sponsor the construction of six new buildings.

The first phase, comprising two buildings and 200+ megawatts, began in June 2024 and is expected to be energized in the first half of 2025.

The second phase, consisting of the six additional buildings and a total of 1.2 gigawatts, began in March 2025 and is expected to be energized in mid-2026.
